name: Raggle of Pork or Lamb
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Pork or Lamb cubes (I've even used turkey cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pan, sear the meat in a little oil over high heat until slightly browned.
2. Turn down the heat and add 1/2 pint of boiling water.
3. Peel and cut the potatoes and onions into bite-sized chunks. Add them to the meat.
4. Cut the mushrooms into chunks and add them along with the bay leaves and finely chopped garlic (if using).
5. Add the peas, both tins of tomato soup, and one tin of water. Stir well.
6. Cook over low heat for about an hour, stirring occasionally.
7. De-seed the peppers and cut into bite-sized chunks. Add to the ragout.
8. Season to taste and cook on low heat for another hour until the potatoes are cooked.
9. Remove the bay leaves.
10. Serve with pieces of crusty bread.
Makes about 4 good servings
Note: I do not know where it originated as I only have a handwritten copy from my grandmother, who was an inventive cook in her own right.
